purpose: Decision-critical reconciliation of the current fee engine and digital-infrastructure optionality.

primary_sources.role: FY2025 operations, revenue, acreage, capital spending, debt, ownership, and risk disclosures

resolved_facts: LandBridge owned or managed more than 315,000 surface acres at year-end 2025 and reports surface-use economic efficiency by legacy, 2024-acquisition, and 2025-acquisition acreage cohorts.
FY2025 revenue was $199.1 million, Adjusted EBITDA $177.2 million, operating cash flow $126.3 million, capital expenditures $4.2 million, and reported Free Cash Flow $122.0 million.

LandBridge states that for prospective digital, power, and similar projects it expects surface-use agreements and does not expect to own or operate the projects or incur significant project capital expenditure.
Publicly disclosed Samsung C&T agreements are options to lease acreage for two possible battery projects totaling 350 MW; option capacity is not operating rent.

partially_answered.finding: The annual report reconciles consolidated revenue, EBITDA, operating cash flow, capital expenditures, Free Cash Flow, and revenue per weighted acre. It does not disclose a contract-by-contract recurring/one-time bridge, incremental margins, maintenance capital, or concentration for each fee activity across a cycle.

The filing supports the low-capital landowner business model and identifies digital infrastructure as a prospective use. The frozen primary record does not disclose an executed Alpha Digital lease/development agreement or LB's net rent, participation, MW phasing, commencement conditions, capital obligations, or termination rights.

unresolved_material_facts: Alpha Digital economics attributable to one LB share cannot be reproduced from an executed agreement in the public packet.
The durability of FY2025 fee growth cannot be separated completely into recurring volume, price, mix, acquisitions, and one-time activity using public segment disclosure.
The Up-C structure, noncontrolling interests, tax distributions, acquisition financing, and future unit issuance require explicit per-share reconciliation in any option realization case.
valuation_controls: Value the disclosed operating platform from normalized owner cash, not a headline acreage multiple.
Assign no contracted-rent status to Alpha Digital without an executed agreement containing LB's net economics.
Keep battery and digital capacity announcements in milestone-gated option components and deduct all financing and ownership claims once.
conclusion: Primary filings establish a capital-light current business but do not meet either follow-up acceptance test. Both gaps remain open and the IC should remain evidence-blocked.
